description:
Advances: mutagen.coverage, mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
1. Extend `MutagenEx.AstCache.load/2` to accept categorised input via an `opts[:categories]` map (e.g. `categories: %{scope: scope_files, test: test_files}`). The first positional arg stays a flat `[String.t()]` list of all files to load. Categorisation is input-only metadata for diagnostics — NOT stored in the entry shape.
2. The cache entry stays a 2-tuple `{Macro.t(), String.t()}` per mutagen.decision.ast_cache_facade_preserved. NO 3-tuple, NO category tag.
3. The `Pipeline.AstCacheFacade` `@callback load/2` signature is PRESERVED VERBATIM. Existing test fakes must continue to compile and work unchanged.
4. `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` `phase_ast_cache` now passes both scope files AND test files. The test set is `test_filter.files` (the cited test files, not the full test tree). Phase order stays unchanged.
5. `Baseline.detect_async_modules/1` (baseline.ex:203-210) reads via `AstCache.get(cache, file)` and consumes the returned `{ast, _source}` directly. On `:error` (cache miss), fall back to the pre-`.25` `File.read/1` path as a safety net — log the miss for diagnostics.
6. Revise `.spec/specs/coverage.spec.md` to document the test-file extension in the AstCache contract (likely a new r-number or a revision to r6).
7. `Baseline` tests gain a case where the cache is populated and `File.read` should NOT be called.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test` green; existing `test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs` still passes; new tests cover the categorised-input path and the fall-back
- Behaviors to demonstrate: Baseline can run without re-reading test files from disk when the cache is hot; cache-miss fall-back works; categorised input produces same entry shape as flat input

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex (F19 descoped; do not touch)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/** (S3 owns)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (S4/S5 own)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex (S1 owns)
- lib/mutagen_ex/beam_cache.ex (S5 owns)
Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/ast_cache_facade.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (phase_ast_cache only)
- lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex
- test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs
- .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md
- CHANGELOG.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT change the `@callback load/2` signature in `pipeline/ast_cache_facade.ex` — per mutagen.decision.ast_cache_facade_preserved this is a hard rule. Verification will grep the callback.
- Do NOT introduce a `files_by_category/2` function. Consumers look up by file path.
- Do NOT add `:persistent_term`-backed caching anywhere — F19 is descoped per mutagen.decision.f19_descoped.
- Do NOT reorder `phase_tests` and `phase_ast_cache` (that ripple effect was explicitly excluded).
- Cache entry shape must stay `{Macro.t(), String.t()}`. If you introduce a category field, the auditor will reject.

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- [ ] `mix test` green
- [ ] Determinism test green
- [ ] Pipeline.AstCacheFacade callback signature unchanged (grep verification)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Commit: `mutagen-wrd.25.02: AstCache categorised load + F18 Baseline cache consumer`

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: 1932fee7-a5d3-4675-b46d-9a3eca264d33
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/1932fee7-a5d3-4675-b46d-9a3eca264d33.jsonl

Commit: 89698cd
Stage branch: mutagen-wrd.25.3/ast-cache-load (based on epic HEAD b343109)
Stage worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-ast-cache-load

Files touched (all within Allowed touches):
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/ast_cache_facade.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (phase_ast_cache function body + its one-line call site in do_pipeline's `with` chain)
- lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex
- test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs
- .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md
- CHANGELOG.md

Tests added (8 new):
- MutagenEx.AstCacheTest (describe "load/2 — r9: categorised input"):
  - "s9a: categorised load produces a cache byte-identical to the flat load"
  - "entry shape stays {ast, source} regardless of category presence (r9)"
  - "reader is still called exactly once per file when :categories is set"
  - "load/2 ignores a malformed :categories value (does not crash)"
- MutagenEx.BaselineTest (describe "r9: ast_cache integration (post-`.25.3`)"):
  - "s9b: cache hit — async warning surfaces without reading the file from disk"
  - "s9b: cache hit with async: false — no warning, and still no disk read"
  - "s9c: cache miss — falls back to File.read/1 + parse"
  - "no ast_cache field at all — fallback path stays intact (pre-`.25` behaviour)"

Verification stubs flipped: mutagen.coverage.v6 (new) added with `execute: true`, covering r9. Existing v3 (covers r6) still passing — the AstCache changes are backwards-compatible.

Hard-rule checks:
- `Pipeline.AstCacheFacade.@callback load/2` signature is byte-identical (grep-verified). No positional change, no return-type change.
- Cache entry shape is `{Macro.t(), String.t()}` 2-tuple. Test asserts `{_ast, source} = entry` matching.
- No `:persistent_term`-backed caching introduced (the only references are pre-existing in mutation_runner telemetry counters).
- No `files_by_category/2` consumer API exists (moduledoc text mentions the name only to say it does not exist).
- Phase order unchanged: phase_tests (line 420) -> phase_ast_cache (line 422) in do_pipeline.
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex untouched (F19 descoped).

Project verification (Phase 1 gate):
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: green
- `mix test --seed 0`: 561 tests, 0 failures (553 previous + 8 new)
- `mix test` (5x, random seed): all green, no flakes attributable to this change

Deferred must requirements:
- Production wiring of `ast_cache: ast_cache` from `phase_ast_cache` through to `Baseline.run/1`'s input map (i.e., editing `phase_baseline` to pass the cache). Strict reading of this ticket's Allowed touches restricts `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` to `phase_ast_cache only`; `phase_baseline` is out-of-scope here. Baseline is FULLY cache-aware module-side (deliverable 5 is met for the module contract), and the new s9b/s9c tests verify both the cache-hit and cache-miss paths by passing `:ast_cache` directly in the input. Until the production wiring lands in a follow-up, the live `mix mutagen` pipeline keeps Baseline's pre-`.25` fallback path (`File.read/1` for each cited test file). I tried to `bw create` a follow-up ticket for this single-line plumbing change but it was blocked by the auto-mode classifier as scope creep — the orchestrator can decide whether to file one or fold the wiring into the next stage.

Summary: Extended `AstCache.load/2` with an optional input-only `:categories` map for diagnostic visibility; kept the cache entry shape and the `Pipeline.AstCacheFacade` `@callback load/2` signature byte-identical per `mutagen.decision.ast_cache_facade_preserved`. Mix task's `phase_ast_cache` now loads scope files + cited test files in one pass with `:categories` set. `Baseline.detect_async_modules/1` consumes the cache via `AstCache.get/2` when present and falls back to `File.read/1` + parse on miss (logged). Spec gains `r9` + scenarios s9a/s9b/s9c + verification v6.

title: S2: AstCache categorised load + F18 Baseline cache consumer
